name: Dell XPS 13 Plus 9320
introText: The Dell XPS 13 Plus is a bold, minimalist ultrabook with a haptic touchpad, capacitive function row, and a stunning OLED display — one of the thinnest and lightest premium Windows laptops available.
whatWorks: 13.4" OLED display with true blacks and vibrant color — one of the best screens available in a 13-inch Windows laptop, ideal for photo and video work.
Exceptionally thin and rigid CNC aluminum chassis — the XPS 13 Plus feels like a premium product that justifies its price in build quality alone.
worthKnowing: Only two Thunderbolt 4 ports — no USB-A, no SD card, no HDMI. A hub is mandatory for any external peripherals.
Battery life trails MacBook Air significantly — 10–12 hours in light use vs. the Air's 18 hours. Heavy use drops it to 6–8 hours.

commonComplaints.title: Poor battery life for the price
commonComplaints.body: At nearly $1,800, many users expect MacBook Air-level endurance. The XPS 13 Plus delivers 8–10 hours in real use — good but not exceptional.

commonComplaints.title: Capacitive function row is divisive
commonComplaints.body: The touch-sensitive row replacing physical F-keys is easy to accidentally activate and lacks tactile feedback. Polarizing for users who rely on F-keys in coding or gaming.

commonComplaints.title: Runs warm under load
commonComplaints.body: The thin chassis limits cooling; sustained CPU workloads cause the bottom to become uncomfortably warm and trigger throttling.

verdict: The Dell XPS 13 Plus is the best-looking Windows ultrabook with one of the finest OLED displays in its class. Battery life and port selection are its main weaknesses — if you need all-day runtime without a charger, the MacBook Air is a better choice. If Windows is non-negotiable and aesthetics matter, the XPS 13 Plus delivers.

realBuyers.quote: The display is incredible for photo editing. Colors are accurate and the OLED contrast makes everything pop.
Beautiful machine but had to buy a USB-C hub immediately. No ports is a real limitation.
Runs warm when I'm compiling code. Throttles after a few minutes. Wish the cooling was better.

metaDescription: Dell XPS 13 Plus 9320 review Canada: Intel Ultra 7, OLED display, 1.26kg. ~$1,799 CAD. Best premium Windows ultrabook for Canadians.
